paper_title: Peruvian Margin Sediment and Oxygen Data Study

methods.method_name: Sediment Core Data Acquisition and Compilation
methods.description: Describes the process of obtaining sediment core data used in the study, combining newly retrieved cores with data from existing literature.

steps.description: 42 new cores recovered during R/V METEOR cruises M77/1 and M77/2 in 2008 were assessed as part of this study (Appendix Table A1).
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve new sediment cores during research cruise
inputs: R/V METEOR cruises M77/1 and M77/2
Peruvian Margin location (Equator to 18° S, 180-2200 m water depth)
outputs: stratigraphic and sedimentological data from new cores

steps.description: Data of 94 cores were taken from the literature
steps.searchable_summary: compile sediment core data from scientific literature
inputs: scientific literature on Peruvian Margin sediment cores
outputs: stratigraphic and sedimentological data from 94 literature cores

steps.description: The Peruvian Margin study is based on stratigraphic and sedimentological data from 136 sediment cores within and below today's OMZ off the western South American continental margin.They are located between the Equator and 18 • S and were retrieved from water depths between 180 and 2200 m.
steps.searchable_summary: combine multiple sediment core datasets into a single study dataset
outputs: combined dataset of stratigraphic and sedimentological data from 136 sediment cores

methods.method_name: Oxygen Concentration Data Acquisition and Selection
methods.description: Describes the process of obtaining and selecting oxygen concentration data from CTD stations along the Peruvian continental margin for environmental interpretation.

steps.description: oxygen concentrations along the Peruvian continental margin were measured during R/V METEOR cruises M77-1, M77-2 (Krahmann, 2012) and M77-3 (Kalvelage et al., 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: measure oxygen concentration using CTD profiler during research cruises
inputs: R/V METEOR cruises M77-1, M77-2, M77-3
outputs: raw oxygen concentration data from CTD stations

steps.searchable_summary: identify relevant CTD stations from dataset
inputs: raw oxygen concentration data from CTD stations

steps.description: with a maximum water depth of 1750 m
steps.searchable_summary: filter CTD stations by maximum water depth
inputs: water depth data for each station
outputs: subset of CTD stations meeting depth criteria

steps.description: and a maximum distance of 175 km to the shore
steps.searchable_summary: filter CTD stations by maximum distance to shore
inputs: subset of CTD stations meeting depth criteria
distance to shore data for each station
outputs: subset of CTD stations meeting depth and distance criteria

steps.description: Other CTD casts further offshore were not included because they already showed significantly elevated oxygen concentrations compared to proximal locations at the same latitude.
steps.searchable_summary: exclude CTD stations with elevated oxygen concentrations at similar latitudes
inputs: subset of CTD stations meeting depth and distance criteria
outputs: final selected set of CTD stations for analysis

steps.description: The oxygen data were not corrected for a 2 µmol kg −1 Figures
steps.searchable_summary: document data correction status for oxygen data
outputs: metadata indicating no correction applied (except for 2 µmol kg −1 offset mentioned)
